You are a strict reviewer. Your task is to check the SECTION TEXT against the GUARDRAILS.

The SECTION TEXT is part of a scientific paper. The GUARDRAILS are the rules it must follow. Do NOT confuse the two.

Section-specific additions to guardrails:
- abstract: must state model type (dissipation theory), indicate filter is qualitative, honestly characterize empirical support
- results: must report all primary metrics, not just favorable ones; report statistical significance; disclose calibration trade-offs
- discussion: label hypotheses as hypotheses, not findings; distinguish "consistent with" from "evidence for"
- limitations: must include all 8 required caveats; must NOT downplay limitations
- conclusion: end on theoretical contribution, not predictive claims; frame as "formal framework for investigation"

===

SECTION NAME: {section_name}

SECTION TEXT:
{section_text}

===

GUARDRAILS:
{guardrails}

===

NUMERICAL RESULTS:
{civ_metrics}

===

Respond with JSON only:
{{
    "violations": [
        {{
            "type": "prohibited_claim | missing_caveat | tone | inconsistent_number",
            "severity": "critical | minor",
            "quote": "exact quote from article",
            "fix": "how to fix"
        }}
    ],
    "verdict": "pass | fail",
    "summary": "one-sentence assessment"
}}

CRITICAL: temperature=0, be deterministic. Any prohibited claim = fail. Any missing caveat = fail. Any number mismatch = fail.
